source: https://langfuse.com/ (static HTML + compiled CSS fetched 2026-07-15; browser extension unavailable, so extraction is from markup/tokens, not live rendering)

color.palette_type: warm paper monochrome — greige/cream neutrals with ink text; one reserved chartreuse CTA yellow, indigo links, and a small set of muted callout hues
color.cta_yellow: #fbff81 — used as a HIGHLIGHTER, not a button fill: swiped behind uppercase mono labels via linear-gradient(transparent 60%, color-mix(in oklab, var(--surface-cta-primary) 75%, transparent) 60% 92%, transparent 92%) like a marker pen, and as thick (stroke-width 11) hand-drawn SVG underline/bracket strokes (seen on /academy)

surfaces_light.note: page body is warm off-white #f6f6f3 (hsl 0 0% 99% shadcn --background over it); panels step down through #edede8 / #e5e5e1; code blocks invert to near-black #333 with cream text #fffcf280

lines_light.note: hairline greige borders (line-structure) do almost all separation; hover/focus promotes borders to the dark line-cta ink; dashed dividers (border-dashed border-line-divider-dash) confirmed on /careers — dashed underlines beneath f37Analog small labels and mono captions, plus tall vertical dashed rules (h-48/h-72 w-px) between columns

callouts.note: muted, slightly desaturated set — periwinkle info instead of blue; plus data-viz accents --muted-green (hsl 84 81% 44%), --muted-magenta (330 90% 70%), --muted-blue (239 84% 67%)

color.code_syntax: on dark #333 code surfaces: orange #c17e2e, pink #d05376, blue #438aa5, secondary text #fffcf280 (translucent cream)

dark_mode.note: dark theme keeps the warm cast (greenish-brown blacks #171714, not neutral gray); CTA yellow darkens to olive #4c4d23; beige accent stays #f1ede1

color.shadcn_layer: a standard shadcn/ui HSL token layer (--background/--foreground/--muted/--destructive etc.) coexists with the custom surface/line/text tokens; --radius is 0.5rem for shadcn components but marketing UI overrides to 2px
color.contrast_strategy: soft-contrast warm ink-on-paper (#222220 on #f6f6f3) rather than pure black-on-white; hierarchy via 4-step text scale (primary/secondary/tertiary/disabled) and surface stepping, color reserved for links, callouts, and code

typography.heading_font: F37 Analog ('f37Analog' + fallback) — a squarish retro-technical serif/slab display face; always used with font-feature-settings 'dlig' on, font-variant-numeric: ordinal, not-italic
typography.body_font: Inter (variable) — workhorse weights are non-standard: 430 body, 450 buttons/labels, 580 emphasis
typography.mono_font: Geist Mono — code blocks, keycap hints, stats

heading_scale.h2_section: 32px / weight 500 / f37Analog (one h2 CTA variant scales 32→44px)
heading_scale.h3: 17px–20px, font-sans medium or f37Analog at text-xl for card titles

body_scale.body_13: 13px / weight 430 / tracking -0.26px — the dominant UI/body size (most-used size on both pages)
body_scale.micro_mono: 9–10px Geist Mono / tracking +0.02em (positive!) — annotation captions: relative timestamps ('2 days ago'), place/date labels ('Berlin', 'March 2023'), 'View All' links; often paired with a dashed bottom border like a filled-in form field

typography.label_variant: f37Analog also appears small: text-sm/medium/leading-110% labels with dashed underlines (careers team roster, annotated figures) — the display face doubles as a caption face
typography.font_style_notes: distinctive pairing: warm retro display serif (F37 Analog with discretionary ligatures) over small, tightly-tracked Inter at odd optical weights (430/450/580); headings often break lines mid-phrase with inline emphasis; leading-trim used to cap-align hero type

spacing.content_density: compact-comfortable — 13px UI type, 26–32px control heights, tight gaps (6px icon gaps)
spacing.section_rhythm: homepage is a continuous ruled document: sections separated by hairline line-structure borders rather than large empty bands; denser than typical dev-tool marketing

layout.grid_system: bordered-panel layout — content areas are hairline-ruled cells (p-px wrappers with bg-line-structure acting as borders), like a printed spec sheet
layout.homepage_structure: top banner (hiring) → nav with mega-menu panels → hero (centered stacked f37Analog headline 'Open Source AI Engineering Platform' + lead + Start free/Documentation buttons with keycap shortcuts) → 'Used by' stats marquee (19 of Fortune 50 / 10B+ observations / 100k engineers, looped 3x) → alternating feature sections with 32px h2s → integrated-platform grid (Observability/Evaluation/Prompt Management/Playground) → integrations wall (SVG logo grid) → enterprise section → 'Why use Langfuse?' accordion list → CTA section → FAQ accordion → footer
layout.sticky_rail: desktop-only 240px sticky right rail: 'Community Stats' (GitHub stars 31.2k, contributors, Q&A threads, latest release) + changelog feed + self-hosting guide links — the marketing page carries live project telemetry
layout.docs_layout: Fumadocs three-column: left sidebar tree, article column, right TOC; same token system as marketing (13px/430 Inter, f37Analog only for page h1s)
layout.docs_shell_reuse: content-heavy marketing pages (/about, /enterprise, /security, /self-hosting, /academy) render inside the docs shell (same scroll-m-28 h1s) instead of bespoke landing layouts — only home, pricing, blog, changelog, customers, careers get marketing treatment

page_patterns.pricing: f37Analog 'Pricing' h1 → Cloud (we host) / Self-hosted (you host) toggle → 4 plan cards using the shadcn layer (plan name text-lg semibold, price text-3xl bold, 'Trusted by' logo rows, hairline-bordered feature lists) → 6 text-sm comparison tables
page_patterns.changelog: single-column feed: entry title + one-sentence description + 10px mono relative timestamp ('2 Days Ago'), media thumbnails; 16px body size appears here
page_patterns.customers: 'User Stories' in f37Analog text-4xl → story cards (company name, headline, italic pull-quote) → giant 'Adopters' table (Company / Use Case / Reference) — proof-by-enumeration
page_patterns.careers: annotated-scrapbook: photo collages with 9-10px mono place/date captions, dashed underlines and vertical dashed rules, f37Analog small labels, founder sign-off ('– Marc, Clemens, Max'), team table with social links, live traction charts

layout.alignment: hero centered; sections otherwise left-aligned within ruled cells

shape.radius_scale: 2px nearly everywhere (rounded-[2px]) — buttons, cards, keycaps, code frames; rounded-full only for avatars/dots; shadcn --radius 0.5rem exists but marketing overrides it
shape.border_usage: constant 1px hairlines in line-structure #cfcfc9; borders are the primary structural device (cells, buttons, keycaps, logo grids); dashed divider token (#bebeb6) for secondary separation
shape.divider_style: solid hairlines for structure; dashed #bebeb6 lines for annotation-level separation (underlines under captions, vertical rules between figure columns) — dashed reads as 'pencil markup' on top of the printed document
shape.rule: near-square corners + visible hairline rules = technical-document look; avoid pill shapes entirely

elevation.shadow_style: very soft, small, layered: buttons/cards use [box-shadow:0_4px_8px_0_rgba(0,0,0,0.05),0_4px_4px_0_rgba(0,0,0,0.03)]; hierarchy mostly from surface stepping (#f6f6f3 → #edede8 → #e5e5e1) and borders, not depth
elevation.depth_cues: surface tone + hairlines first, whisper shadows second; no glass, no glow

iconography.style: Lucide stroke icons at small sizes; large third-party integration logos as flat SVGs in ruled grid cells

motion.easing_durations: transition-colors at 150/180/200ms for most state changes; 700ms for slow reveals
motion.keyframes_observed: marquee / marquee-left / marquee-right / marquee-vertical (stat + logo loops), border-beam (animated border highlight), accordion-down/up, ask-ai-open/close, rive-text-in, tooltip-in, ellipsis, fall, grid, slide, spin-custom
motion.philosophy: functional micro-motion plus a few signature loops (marquees, border-beam); no scroll-scrubbing, no GSAP/Framer/Three (verified absent from page)

components.button_primary: ink-filled: bg #222220 (text-primary token used as fill), cream #f6f6f3 label, 32px height, 2px radius, 12px/450 Inter; embeds a 20x20px keycap showing its keyboard shortcut (e.g. 'S' for Start free) with aria-keyshortcuts
components.button_secondary: surface fill + hairline border + the layered soft shadow, 26–32px height, 2px radius, also with keycap shortcut hint ('G' Get Demo, 'D' Documentation, 'L' Launch App)
components.cta_yellow_reserved: --surface-cta-primary #fbff81 (olive #4c4d23 dark) is never a button fill — it appears as highlighter swipes behind small uppercase labels ('ONLINE'/'OFFLINE' badges on academy courses) and as hand-drawn thick SVG strokes; treat it as marker ink on the paper, not as a fill color
components.highlighter_badge: signature motif: tiny uppercase label with a chartreuse marker-swipe rendered via layered linear-gradient (transparent above 60% and below 92% of the line) + color-mix at 75% opacity, padding 0 4px, negative left margin — imitates a physical highlighter stroke that doesn't quite cover the full text height
components.annotation_captions: 9-10px Geist Mono, +0.02em tracking, text-secondary, min-width with dashed bottom border — used like typewritten labels on an annotated document (dates, places, timestamps)
components.pricing_cards: the one area leaning on the stock shadcn layer: text-lg semibold plan names, text-3xl bold prices, text-sm comparison tables, standard card borders — less bespoke than the marketing pages
components.links: body links underlined with underline-offset-2 and decoration in line-structure greige, darkening on hover; nav links 13px/430 tracking -0.26px in text-tertiary, some with a [text-shadow:0_0_0_#B5AFEA] periwinkle emboss trick
components.keycap: signature component: bordered 2px-radius 20px square, translucent fill (rgba(64,61,57,.3/.4) on dark buttons), Geist-adjacent 12px glyph — keyboard-first affordance repeated across nav and CTAs
components.code_blocks: inverted near-black #333 panels with cream translucent secondary text, muted orange/pink/blue syntax, 1px #333 border, copy buttons on #51504f
components.accordions: FAQ and 'Why use Langfuse?' as h3 accordion rows with accordion-down/up animation
components.stats_cards: big numerals + small greige labels inside ruled cells (Community Stats rail, Used-by marquee)
components.navigation: mega-menu dropdown panels containing rich preview cards (doc guide card, ClickHouse announcement card)

design_style.mood: warm, utilitarian, engineered, understated, slightly retro
design_style.personality: a meticulously typeset technical datasheet — hairline-ruled paper, ink text, retro slab display type, keyboard shortcuts on every button; branding by craft and telemetry (live GitHub stats on the homepage) instead of decoration
design_style.genre: open-source developer-infrastructure marketing, 'warm paper' school (Anthropic/Linear-adjacent warmth rather than dark-mode neon) — distinct from the grayscale Vercel school via cream surfaces, serif display, and visible rules
design_style.visual_language: everything lives in bordered cells on warm paper; text does the hierarchy (4-step ink scale); the only loud element is the chartreuse #fbff81 applied like a physical highlighter pen; product screenshots and a Rive animation supply imagery; integration logos and the Adopters table form walls of proof
design_style.document_metaphor: the whole site behaves like one annotated engineering document: solid hairlines are the printed ruling, dashed lines are pencil markup, 9-10px mono captions are typewritten labels, f37Analog is the letterhead, and chartreuse highlighter strokes mark what matters — most visible on /careers and /academy
design_style.composition: centered hero over a continuous ruled-grid document; a sticky data rail keeps community stats visible; sections alternate copy and product visuals; marquee bands provide horizontal motion breaks
design_style.copy_voice: plain, confident, metric-forward ('19 of Fortune 50', '10+ billion observations/month'); imperative section verbs ('Launch, observe, improve — repeat.'); developer-literal CTAs (Start free, Get Demo, Documentation) each with a keyboard shortcut; personal where it matters (careers ends with a founders' sign-off '– Marc, Clemens, Max'); customer proof via italic pull-quotes and an exhaustive public Adopters table
design_style.interaction_feel: quick color transitions (150–200ms), keyboard-first affordances, hover promotes greige hairlines to ink (border line-structure → line-cta); accordion and marquee are the main moving parts

overview.performance_tier: lightweight-medium — CSS keyframes plus one Rive (canvas) hero animation with a preloaded PNG mock as placeholder; no WebGL/Three.js/GSAP/Framer Motion detected
overview.composite_notes: Extraction was from static HTML/CSS (no live browser), so runtime behavior of the Rive scene, marquee speeds, and border-beam placement are inferred from shipped keyframes, class names, and the preloaded rive-mock.png rather than observed. Verify live before cloning motion details.

rive_animation.details: hero/product illustration driven by the Rive runtime (rive-mock.png preloaded at up to 2048w as the static placeholder; rive-text-in keyframe for entrance)

marquee_loops.details: horizontal (and a vertical variant) infinite marquees: the 'Used by' stats band is duplicated 3x in the DOM for seamless looping; marquee-left/right/vertical keyframes shipped

border_beam.details: border-beam keyframe — an animated highlight traveling along an element's border, used as a spotlight accent

highlighter_strokes.details: chartreuse marker effects: CSS gradient highlighter swipes behind labels (layered linear-gradient + color-mix, covering only 60–92% of the line height) and inline SVG paths stroked in var(--surface-cta-primary) at stroke-width 11 with butt caps/miter joins — hand-drawn underline/bracket annotations (seen on /academy)

video.details: one product video (in-app agent demo, mp4 from static.langfuse.com)

scroll_effects.note: no parallax or scroll-scrub libraries; at most standard reveal transitions

cursor_effects.note: --cursor-x/--cursor-y custom properties exist in docs CSS (likely a hover-gradient card) but no custom cursor

glassmorphism.note: opaque warm surfaces throughout; translucency limited to button-grey rgba fills

text_effects.note: headlines static; only rive-text-in entrance and the text-shadow emboss on nav links
